outcome.headline: Agent looped re-checking board orientation, ran a Stockfish subprocess that hung ~5 min, and timed out without ever writing /app/answer.txt
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Normalized (lowercase/trim) string equality between /app/answer.txt and expected_answer.txt ('Rd5'); missing file scores 0
outcome.what_agent_produced: No /app/answer.txt was ever written; the agent timed out mid-analysis after 600s. The correct move d8d5 (Rd5) transiently appeared in its own Stockfish output but was never identified or committed
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent re-analysis until timeout
failure_modes.description: After the first Stockfish run failed to show a winning move, the agent kept re-running essentially the same analysis (higher depth, mate-search, multipv, board rotation, Lichess cloud eval) instead of committing any answer to /app/answer.txt, exhausting the 600s budget and triggering AgentTimeoutError.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: bestmove d4f5 ponder d3f5

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent board reinterpretation
failure_modes.description: After the engine result did not fit its expectation, the agent repeatedly changed or questioned the board orientation and piece identities, including testing a rotated FEN, consuming the remaining budget without converging on a submitted move.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Correcting FEN Interpretation: I've been working to get the correct interpretation of the FEN string. I initially thought the board might be rotated, leading to misinterpretations of the files and ranks. Now, I've confirmed that the standard orientation is assumed with 'a1' at the bottom-left. Following this, I've re-read the FEN and found the mistake: the White King is on a8, not h1.

failure_modes.name: Correct engine candidate not converted to answer
failure_modes.description: Stockfish output exposed the canonical move as d8d5, which corresponds to the expected algebraic answer Rd5, but the agent treated the low centipawn score as a contradiction and kept searching instead of writing the answer.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: info depth 7 seldepth 6 multipv 1 score cp 367 nodes 781 nps 260333 hashfull 0 tbhits 0 time 3 pv d8d5

counterfactual.X: At step 8 or 9, convert Stockfish's d8d5 candidate to algebraic notation and write `Rd5` to /app/answer.txt.
counterfactual.Y: The exact-answer verifier would have matched the expected answer Rd5.

failure_modes.name: Long-running malformed Stockfish probe consumed budget
failure_modes.description: The agent launched a MultiPV-style Stockfish check that blocked until the harness cancelled it, wasting most of the remaining task time and contributing directly to the final timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Command was automatically cancelled because it exceeded the timeout of 5.0 minutes without output.

failure_modes.name: Misencoded board orientation
failure_modes.description: The agent mapped the labeled image (h-file on the left, row 1 at top) directly into a standard FEN without reconciling orientation, then treated Stockfish’s small positive scores as proof the puzzle had no forced win. Early engine output included pv d8d5 at depth 7, but the agent fixated on bestmove d4f5 from deeper searches instead of converting d8d5 to algebraic Rd5.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: bestmove d4f5 ponder d3f5

failure_modes.name: Image-to-FEN misperception
failure_modes.description: Agent parsed the puzzle image into a FEN that does not match the actual position; Stockfish therefore evaluated the position as roughly equal (cp ~13) and never returned the puzzle answer Rd5 (uci d8d5). The whole subsequent analysis was rooted in this wrong board state.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: fen = "3r2k1/pp3pp1/4b2p/7Q/3n4/PqBBR2P/5PP1/7K b - - 0 1"

counterfactual.X: After Stockfish reported only a +0.1 cp evaluation on a position the prompt guarantees is winning for Black, treat that as a contradiction and re-derive piece placement from the image (or use OCR / python-chess to double-check) before continuing.
counterfactual.Y: A corrected FEN would surface Rd5 (d8d5) as the winning move, which the agent could then write to /app/answer.txt.

failure_modes.name: Vision-driven FEN misidentification
failure_modes.description: The agent's reconstructed FEN never yielded a winning evaluation, indicating at least one piece was misread from the image; the agent flip-flopped on board orientation ('h at the bottom-left' vs 'a8 at the bottom-right') and on whether the b3 piece was a queen/rook/bishop, but never reconciled the contradiction with the task's stated guarantee.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: could the Queen on b3 actually be a Black Bishop?

failure_modes.name: Wrong board state decoded from the image (FEN misperception)
failure_modes.description: A2/B2/C2.4 — The agent reconstructed the position by reading the PNG and produced a FEN that does not contain the forced mate the task guarantees. Because the analyzed board state was wrong, Stockfish never surfaced Rd5#, and every downstream move evaluation (Nf5/d4f5, Qd1, Qxc3) was reasoning over the wrong position. The agent repeatedly suspected specific piece misreads but could not resolve them.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: considering if the pieces on b3, a3 and h5 could be different, like a black knight instead of queen, but the piece has a crown

counterfactual.X: Correct the single misidentified piece so the FEN matches the true position
counterfactual.Y: Stockfish reports the forced mate, the agent identifies Rd5 and writes it to /app/answer.txt, passing the verifier

failure_modes.name: Misread board into FEN
failure_modes.description: The agent decoded the diagram with h1 as top-left and built FEN 3r2k1/pp3pp1/4b2p/7Q/3n4/PqBBR2P/5PP1/7K b - - 0 1. Stockfish on that position never shows a forced win (depth 35 bestmove d4f5, score cp 8), so the agent never converged on the required Rd5.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Top-left is h1. Bottom-right is a8.

failure_modes.name: Wrong FEN from image
failure_modes.description: The agent manually decoded the PNG into an incorrect position (e.g., Black queen on b3) and fed that FEN to Stockfish, which evaluated the position as roughly equal rather than a forced black win.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: b3: Black Queen

failure_modes.name: Vision/transcription error built a FEN with no winning move for Black
failure_modes.description: The agent transcribed the PNG into FEN 3r2k1/pp3pp1/4b2p/7Q/3n4/PqBBR2P/5PP1/7K which Stockfish evaluates as ~+0.08 for White — a position with no forced win for Black, contradicting the task's guarantee. This wrong board became the substrate for every downstream search, and the agent oscillated between trusting Stockfish, suspecting individual squares (was h5 really a queen? is b3 really a queen?), and even trying a 'rotated' FEN — never identifying the actual misread.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The initial +0.13 score suggests a slight white advantage or a draw, which directly contradicts the winning requirement for black.

failure_modes.name: Five-minute Stockfish hang
failure_modes.description: A multipv Stockfish subprocess at step 12 was auto-cancelled after 5.0 minutes without finishing, consuming roughly half the 600s agent budget on non-delivering analysis.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Command was automatically cancelled because it exceeded the timeout of 5.0 minutes without output.

failure_modes.name: Never wrote answer file
failure_modes.description: The task explicitly requires writing the final algebraic move to /app/answer.txt, but the trajectory contains no write to that path and ends on a text-only agent step when the harness raises AgentTimeoutError.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: When you have determined the final answer, write it to `/app/answer.txt`.

counterfactual.X: After Stockfish PV showed d8d5, run `echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt`
counterfactual.Y: Continued open-ended board re-analysis and Stockfish runs without writing any answer file

failure_modes.name: Misinterpreted validation signal from engine output
failure_modes.description: The agent ran meaningful validation with Stockfish but treated the engine's low evaluation and alternate best move as evidence that the position/FEN was wrong, instead of converging on the puzzle answer. The correct UCI move d8d5 appeared in its own Stockfish output, but it never converted that to algebraic Rd5 or wrote the answer file.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: info depth 9 seldepth 11 multipv 1 score cp 44 nodes 5001 nps 555666 hashfull 1 tbhits 0 time 9 pv d8d5 h5h4 b3b6 h4g3 b6d6 g3d6 d5d6

counterfactual.X: At the point d8d5 appeared in the engine line, convert it to algebraic notation as Rd5 and write exactly that to /app/answer.txt.
counterfactual.Y: The verifier's exact normalized comparison against expected_answer.txt would have passed.

failure_modes.name: Misread validation signal from engine analysis
failure_modes.description: The agent treated Stockfish's near-equal evaluation on its constructed FEN as evidence that the task/FEN must be wrong, even though the expected move appeared in Stockfish principal variations as d8d5, and continued searching rather than converting the candidate to algebraic notation Rd5.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Stockfish's evaluation of +0.08 seems to contradict the requirement for a guaranteed win for Black.

counterfactual.X: At the engine-output step, recognize d8d5 as rook move Rd5 and write echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt.
counterfactual.Y: The exact-string verifier would have matched the expected answer Rd5.

failure_modes.name: Five-minute Stockfish hang
failure_modes.description: At step 12 the agent launched `go depth 12 multipv 15`; the shell command was auto-cancelled after 5 minutes without finishing, consuming most remaining budget before the 600s agent timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Command was automatically cancelled because it exceeded the timeout of 5.0 minutes without output.

failure_modes.name: Hung subprocess command drained the entire time budget
failure_modes.description: At step 12 the agent ran a Python/Stockfish 'go depth 12 multipv 15' script whose stdout-reading and terminate logic deadlocked; the shell command hung until cancelled after 5 minutes, consuming essentially all remaining agent time and directly causing the 600s AgentTimeoutError
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Command was automatically cancelled because it exceeded the timeout of 5.0 minutes without output.

counterfactual.X: Use a bounded invocation (e.g. stockfish 'go movetime 2000' or a subprocess call with a hard kill/timeout and proper pipe draining) instead of an unbounded multipv read loop that deadlocks
counterfactual.Y: The command returns in seconds, preserving ~5 minutes of budget for the agent to commit an answer and write /app/answer.txt

failure_modes.name: No answer delivered
failure_modes.description: Despite the instruction to write the final move to /app/answer.txt, the agent spent the full 600s budget re-running analysis and orientation checks, then hit AgentTimeoutError without ever submitting an answer.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I'm going to run Stockfish again, but this time with a white turn to see if the evaluation changes significantly.

counterfactual.X: Write the identified winning move (Rd5) to /app/answer.txt, e.g. echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt
counterfactual.Y: Continued open-ended re-analysis and Stockfish runs until the harness timed out at 600 seconds without writing /app/answer.txt

failure_modes.name: Never wrote answer file
failure_modes.description: Despite instruction.md requiring a single move in /app/answer.txt, the agent spent the full 600s budget on engine installs and analysis loops, then hit AgentTimeoutError on step 18 without ever echoing a final move. The verifier’s first gate is file existence (test.sh:11-14).
failure_modes.evidence_quote: When you have determined the final answer, write it to `/app/answer.txt`.

counterfactual.X: Run `echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt` once the winning rook move is identified (Stockfish shallow PV d8d5 matches expected Rd5).
counterfactual.Y: Continued Stockfish re-analysis and timed out without creating /app/answer.txt.

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent attempted legitimate Stockfish analysis on its constructed FEN; no attempt to bypass the verifier or read /tests/expected_answer.txt.

notes_for_aggregation: Vision/perception failure on a chess-image GAIA task: VLM misidentified piece placement → wrong FEN → solver-tool consensus that contradicted the prompt's guaranteed-win premise → agent looped on re-analysis and timed out without writing any answer.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier is a plain normalized exact-string compare of /app/answer.txt against an unreadable /tests/expected_answer.txt. No partial-credit surface, no path traversal hook, no judge-LLM to manipulate.

outcome.what_verifier_checked: Case-insensitive string equality between /app/answer.txt and the expected answer 'Rd5'.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergence: endless board re-interpretation, never committed an answer
failure_modes.description: Across steps 3,4,8,9,10,13,16,17 the agent repeatedly second-guessed the board orientation and piece identities because Stockfish reported a near-equal eval contradicting the 'guaranteed win' premise. It never trusted a reading, never wrote any answer to /app/answer.txt (despite the explicit output requirement and despite d8d5/Rd5 appearing in its own output), and cycled without approaching a deliverable
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I've made a crucial correction regarding the board's orientation; it was reversed in my mind.

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent attempted genuine analysis via Stockfish and python-chess; it tried a lichess cloud-eval lookup but it was rate-limited (HTTP 429) and no online solution was used. No answer was ever written, so no gaming of the verifier occurred

issues: Verifier is plain normalized string equality against 'Rd5'; the configured JUDGE_MODELS env vars are unused by test.sh

The position when transcribed to FEN evaluates only ~+0.08 in Stockfish 15.1, which does not present as a 'guaranteed win'; a solver relying on engine eval gets no clear signal toward the canonical answer Rd5, demanding exact image transcription and material-win reasoning
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Exact-match comparison to a fixed expected answer; not LLM-judge based, so no rubric-gaming surface, though a known GAIA answer could be guessed without solving

outcome.headline: Agent misread the chess image into a wrong FEN, Stockfish scored it as drawn (+0.08), and the agent looped endlessly re-verifying piece placement until the 600s timeout — never writing /app/answer.txt and never considering the gold move Rd5.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Whether /app/answer.txt, lowercased and whitespace-trimmed, exactly equals the expected answer 'rd5'.
outcome.what_agent_produced: No answer file at all; the agent never wrote /app/answer.txt and was killed by the agent timeout while still re-checking its FEN. The only moves it ever evaluated were d4f5 (Nf5), Qd1, and Qxc3 — never Rd5.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent re-verification loop ending in timeout
failure_modes.description: A5/B1/C6.5 — After Stockfish reported a near-draw evaluation that contradicted the 'guaranteed win' premise, the agent correctly inferred its FEN must be wrong but then spent the rest of the budget re-checking piece placement square-by-square, second-guessing orientation, swapping suspected pieces, and re-running Stockfish without ever converging on a corrected position or committing an answer. It hit rate limiting and the 600s timeout with no answer.txt written.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The initial +0.13 score suggests a slight white advantage or a draw, which directly contradicts the winning requirement for black. I'm starting to suspect that there might be a problem with the FEN.

failure_modes.name: Wrong board state decoded from the image (FEN misperception)
failure_modes.description: A2/B2/C2.4 — The agent reconstructed the position by reading the PNG and produced a FEN that does not contain the forced mate the task guarantees. Because the analyzed board state was wrong, Stockfish never surfaced Rd5#, and every downstream move evaluation (Nf5/d4f5, Qd1, Qxc3) was reasoning over the wrong position. The agent repeatedly suspected specific piece misreads but could not resolve them.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: considering if the pieces on b3, a3 and h5 could be different, like a black knight instead of queen, but the piece has a crown

counterfactual.X: Correct the single misidentified piece so the FEN matches the true position
counterfactual.Y: Stockfish reports the forced mate, the agent identifies Rd5 and writes it to /app/answer.txt, passing the verifier

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent attempted a legitimate solve via image decoding plus Stockfish analysis; it never accessed the expected answer, never wrote a placeholder, and produced no output at all. No gaming behavior.

issues: Verifier normalizes only case and whitespace; the standard algebraic notation for this mating move is 'Rd5#', which would NOT match the expected 'rd5' since the '#' is not stripped — a chess-correct answer could be marked wrong.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier is an exact normalized string comparison against a hidden expected_answer.txt; the agent cannot read the expected value, so there is no structural exploit. The only weakness is brittle normalization of notation suffixes (#/+), not a hackability vector.

outcome.headline: Agent built a wrong FEN from the image, then looped on re-verifying it while running depth-22/35 Stockfish searches; the 600s budget ran out before /app/answer.txt was ever written.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Exact normalized string match between /app/answer.txt and /tests/expected_answer.txt ('Rd5').
outcome.what_agent_produced: No /app/answer.txt was ever written (zero write_file calls); the leading candidate during the loop was Nf5 (d4f5) at ~+0.08, not Rd5. Agent terminated by harness timeout at step 17.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Endless FEN re-verification loop, no answer file written before 600s timeout
failure_modes.description: After Stockfish's +0.13/+0.08 evaluation contradicted the puzzle's 'guarantees a win' premise, the agent re-examined the board orientation and piece identities over and over (steps 8,9,10,13,16,17), re-ran expensive depth-22/35/mate-10 Stockfish searches (one was auto-cancelled after 5 minutes of stdout drain), and never committed any move to /app/answer.txt. No write_file call occurred in the entire trajectory.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I'm systematically verifying each piece's placement within the provided FEN to identify potential discrepancies.

counterfactual.X: Write the current best candidate to /app/answer.txt (e.g. 'Rd5') before continuing analysis, and cap Stockfish calls to a short fixed budget.
counterfactual.Y: Would have produced *some* answer instead of an empty file, but the candidate move the agent was actually converging on (Nf5) is wrong, so a one-step fix alone would not yield reward=1 — the FEN itself had to be corrected.

failure_modes.name: Vision/transcription error built a FEN with no winning move for Black
failure_modes.description: The agent transcribed the PNG into FEN 3r2k1/pp3pp1/4b2p/7Q/3n4/PqBBR2P/5PP1/7K which Stockfish evaluates as ~+0.08 for White — a position with no forced win for Black, contradicting the task's guarantee. This wrong board became the substrate for every downstream search, and the agent oscillated between trusting Stockfish, suspecting individual squares (was h5 really a queen? is b3 really a queen?), and even trying a 'rotated' FEN — never identifying the actual misread.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The initial +0.13 score suggests a slight white advantage or a draw, which directly contradicts the winning requirement for black.

reward_hacking.evidence: No attempt to write the expected answer directly, edit the verifier, or read /tests/expected_answer.txt. One step (16) tried lichess.org's cloud-eval API for an engine hint — a soft violation of 'Do not cheat by using online solutions' — but the call failed with HTTP 429 and produced no usable data, so the verifier was not gamed.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier is a plain normalized exact-string compare of /app/answer.txt against an unreadable /tests/expected_answer.txt. No partial-credit surface, no path traversal hook, no judge-LLM to manipulate.

outcome.headline: Agent looped on FEN re-verification, never committed an answer, and timed out at 600s with /app/answer.txt unwritten (reward 0.0)

outcome.what_agent_produced: No /app/answer.txt was written; the agent ran out of time mid-analysis

failure_modes.name: Non-convergence on board interpretation

failure_modes.evidence_quote: Stockfish's evaluation of the position as drawn (+0.08). The prompt's winning guarantee means a Stockfish-considered move at depth 35 exists!

counterfactual.Y: A committed answer in /app/answer.txt would have been scored by the verifier (still 0 unless Rd5, but it eliminates the timeout failure mode and gives a chance at the correct move)

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier does a normalized string compare against a single expected token; no leak path observed in /tests since /tests is not mounted into /app for the agent.

outcome.headline: The agent reconstructed and analyzed the chess position but misinterpreted Stockfish output, repeatedly re-opened the board-orientation question, and timed out without writing /app/answer.txt.

outcome.what_agent_produced: The trajectory contains no command writing /app/answer.txt; the agent was still analyzing FEN orientation, Stockfish lines, and alternative candidate moves when the 600 second agent timeout occurred.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Misinterpreted validation signal from engine output
failure_modes.description: The agent ran meaningful validation with Stockfish but treated the engine's low evaluation and alternate best move as evidence that the position/FEN was wrong, instead of converging on the puzzle answer. The correct UCI move d8d5 appeared in its own Stockfish output, but it never converted that to algebraic Rd5 or wrote the answer file.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: info depth 9 seldepth 11 multipv 1 score cp 44 nodes 5001 nps 555666 hashfull 1 tbhits 0 time 9 pv d8d5 h5h4 b3b6 h4g3 b6d6 g3d6 d5d6

counterfactual.X: At the point d8d5 appeared in the engine line, convert it to algebraic notation as Rd5 and write exactly that to /app/answer.txt.
counterfactual.Y: The verifier's exact normalized comparison against expected_answer.txt would have passed.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent rechecking loop until timeout
failure_modes.description: After the validation conflict, the agent repeatedly rechecked board orientation, FEN variants, candidate tactics, and external/cloud evaluation rather than committing to an answer. This consumed the remaining budget and caused the final timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Command was automatically cancelled because it exceeded the timeout of 5.0 minutes without output.

reward_hacking.evidence: The task said not to use online hints specific to the task, but the trajectory includes a tool call described as "Check lichess cloud eval" and the code string "https://lichess.org/api/cloud-eval?fen="; it failed with HTTP 429 and did not affect the reward.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The verifier is a simple exact-answer comparison, but no evidence in the task files shows the expected answer is exposed to the agent runtime; it is adequate for this short-answer GAIA task.

outcome.headline: Agent decoded much of the chess position and used Stockfish, but then cycled on board-orientation and candidate-move doubts until the 600s timeout without writing an answer.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The verifier checked that /app/answer.txt exists, read it as a single string, normalized lowercase/trimmed whitespace, and compared it exactly to /tests/expected_answer.txt, which is Rd5.
outcome.what_agent_produced: No submitted answer file was produced. The agent built FENs, ran Stockfish repeatedly, saw engine lines containing d8d5, explored wrong alternatives such as Qd1+ and a rotated FEN, and timed out before writing /app/answer.txt.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent chess analysis loop
failure_modes.description: After the first useful FEN and engine runs, the agent kept rechecking piece identities, board orientation, alternate FENs, and speculative tactics instead of selecting a move and writing the answer, exhausting the task time budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I'm systematically verifying each piece's placement within the provided FEN to identify potential discrepancies.

counterfactual.X: The agent would need to stop the repeated reinterpretation loop and commit to a concrete move selection strategy before timeout.
counterfactual.Y: It would have produced an answer file instead of timing out with no submission.

failure_modes.name: Misread validation signal from engine analysis
failure_modes.description: The agent treated Stockfish's near-equal evaluation on its constructed FEN as evidence that the task/FEN must be wrong, even though the expected move appeared in Stockfish principal variations as d8d5, and continued searching rather than converting the candidate to algebraic notation Rd5.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Stockfish's evaluation of +0.08 seems to contradict the requirement for a guaranteed win for Black.

counterfactual.X: At the engine-output step, recognize d8d5 as rook move Rd5 and write echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt.
counterfactual.Y: The exact-string verifier would have matched the expected answer Rd5.

reward_hacking.evidence: The task said not to use online solutions or task-specific hints, but the trajectory includes a tool call described as "Check lichess cloud eval" and it returned "Error: HTTP Error 429: Too Many Requests". There was no verifier manipulation.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The verifier is an exact normalized string comparison against expected_answer.txt; the Dockerfile copies only the image into /app/files, so the expected answer is not exposed by the agent environment described in the task sources.

outcome.headline: The agent decoded the board and repeatedly used Stockfish, but never converged or wrote an answer before timing out.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The verifier checks whether /app/answer.txt exists, then lowercases/trims it and compares it exactly with the expected answer Rd5; missing or mismatched answers receive reward 0.
outcome.what_agent_produced: No final answer was written to /app/answer.txt; the trajectory ended in analysis and tool use under AgentTimeoutError.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent board interpretation loop
failure_modes.description: After constructing a plausible FEN and seeing candidate engine lines including the expected rook move, the agent kept re-questioning board orientation and piece identity instead of committing to a move, exhausting the time budget without producing an answer.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I'm now zeroing in on verifying the positions of key pieces, particularly the white and black Queens, and the pawn on h6.

counterfactual.X: At step 13, stop the speculative re-analysis and write the already surfaced candidate as algebraic notation: echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt.
counterfactual.Y: The verifier would have found /app/answer.txt containing the exact expected answer Rd5 instead of timing out with no submitted answer.

failure_modes.name: Hanging Stockfish validation command
failure_modes.description: A validation script intended to inspect multiple top moves blocked until the harness cancelled it after five minutes, consuming the remaining budget during an already non-convergent search.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Command was automatically cancelled because it exceeded the timeout of 5.0 minutes without output.

reward_hacking.evidence: The trajectory shows chess image analysis, local Stockfish use, and a failed Lichess cloud-eval call; it does not show attempts to inspect verifier files, expected answers, or manipulate scoring.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The Dockerfile copies only the image workspace into /app/files; the expected answer is in the verifier-side tests and is not copied into the agent environment by the task Dockerfile.

outcome.headline: The agent hit the 600s timeout while endlessly re-verifying its hand-built FEN after Stockfish scored the position as ~drawish, and never wrote /app/answer.txt.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh:11-14 requires /app/answer.txt to exist; test.sh:29-34 compares normalized agent answer to /tests/expected_answer.txt (Rd5).
outcome.what_agent_produced: No /app/answer.txt; the agent installed Stockfish, analyzed FEN 3r2k1/pp3pp1/4b2p/7Q/3n4/PqBBR2P/5PP1/7K b - - 0 1, and timed out mid re-verification.

failure_modes.name: Draw-eval FEN reverify loop
failure_modes.description: After Stockfish returned shallow centipawn scores (~+0.08) and bestmove d4f5 at depth 35, the agent treated a winning-move task as a FEN transcription bug and spent remaining steps re-checking pieces instead of writing an answer.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Stockfish's evaluation of the position as drawn (+0.08).

counterfactual.X: After depth-7 PV listed d8d5, run echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt
counterfactual.Y: Ran deeper Stockfish and piece-by-piece FEN reverification until agent timeout

failure_modes.name: Five-minute Stockfish hang
failure_modes.description: A multipv Stockfish subprocess at step 12 was auto-cancelled after 5.0 minutes without finishing, consuming roughly half the 600s agent budget on non-delivering analysis.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Command was automatically cancelled because it exceeded the timeout of 5.0 minutes without output.

reward_hacking.evidence: Step 16 queried the Lichess cloud-eval API despite instruction.md:32 forbidding online solutions/hints.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier is an exact string match against expected_answer.txt; no path to pass without the correct move string.

outcome.headline: The agent hit the 600s harness timeout without ever writing /app/answer.txt, despite Stockfish briefly listing d8d5 (Rd5) in a cancelled multipv search.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh:11-34 requires /app/answer.txt to exist and match /tests/expected_answer.txt (Rd5) after lowercasing/trimming; missing file prints ERROR at lines 11-14
outcome.what_agent_produced: No /app/answer.txt; decoded the PNG into FEN 3r2k1/pp3pp1/4b2p/7Q/3n4/PqBBR2P/5PP1/7K b - - 0 1 and ran Stockfish (primary bestmove d4f5)

failure_modes.name: Never wrote answer file
failure_modes.description: The task explicitly requires writing the final algebraic move to /app/answer.txt, but the trajectory contains no write to that path and ends on a text-only agent step when the harness raises AgentTimeoutError.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: When you have determined the final answer, write it to `/app/answer.txt`.

counterfactual.X: After Stockfish PV showed d8d5, run `echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt`
counterfactual.Y: Continued open-ended board re-analysis and Stockfish runs without writing any answer file

failure_modes.name: Five-minute Stockfish hang
failure_modes.description: At step 12 the agent launched `go depth 12 multipv 15`; the shell command was auto-cancelled after 5 minutes without finishing, consuming most remaining budget before the 600s agent timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Command was automatically cancelled because it exceeded the timeout of 5.0 minutes without output.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier is an exact string match against a fixed expected_answer.txt; solving requires reading the board image, not bypassing tests.

outcome.headline: The agent timed out after building an incorrect FEN from the board image and never wrote the required winning move to /app/answer.txt.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: tests/test.sh:11-14 requires /app/answer.txt to exist; tests/test.sh:29-34 compares normalized agent answer to /tests/expected_answer.txt (expected "Rd5").
outcome.what_agent_produced: No /app/answer.txt; repeated Stockfish/python-chess analysis on FEN "3r2k1/pp3pp1/4b2p/7Q/3n4/PqBBR2P/5PP1/7K b - - 0 1" yielding bestmove d4f5 (~draw), not Rd5.

failure_modes.name: Wrong FEN from image
failure_modes.description: The agent manually decoded the PNG into an incorrect position (e.g., Black queen on b3) and fed that FEN to Stockfish, which evaluated the position as roughly equal rather than a forced black win.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: b3: Black Queen

failure_modes.name: No answer delivered
failure_modes.description: Despite the instruction to write the final move to /app/answer.txt, the agent spent the full 600s budget re-running analysis and orientation checks, then hit AgentTimeoutError without ever submitting an answer.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I'm going to run Stockfish again, but this time with a white turn to see if the evaluation changes significantly.

counterfactual.X: Write the identified winning move (Rd5) to /app/answer.txt, e.g. echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt
counterfactual.Y: Continued open-ended re-analysis and Stockfish runs until the harness timed out at 600 seconds without writing /app/answer.txt

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier is a direct string match against /tests/expected_answer.txt; the agent did not access /tests/ or expected_answer.txt in the trajectory.

outcome.headline: The agent timed out after repeated Stockfish runs on a misread board encoding and never wrote /app/answer.txt with the winning move Rd5.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: tests/test.sh:11-14 exits 0 with reward 0 if /app/answer.txt is missing; tests/test.sh:18-34 normalize and exact-match agent answer against tests/expected_answer.txt (Rd5)
outcome.what_agent_produced: No /app/answer.txt; trajectory shows a self-built FEN 3r2k1/pp3pp1/4b2p/7Q/3n4/PqBBR2P/5PP1/7K b - - 0 1 and Stockfish bestmove d4f5 (Nxf5) with ~+0.07 cp, not the expected Rd5

failure_modes.name: Misencoded board orientation
failure_modes.description: The agent mapped the labeled image (h-file on the left, row 1 at top) directly into a standard FEN without reconciling orientation, then treated Stockfish’s small positive scores as proof the puzzle had no forced win. Early engine output included pv d8d5 at depth 7, but the agent fixated on bestmove d4f5 from deeper searches instead of converting d8d5 to algebraic Rd5.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: bestmove d4f5 ponder d3f5

failure_modes.name: Never wrote answer file
failure_modes.description: Despite instruction.md requiring a single move in /app/answer.txt, the agent spent the full 600s budget on engine installs and analysis loops, then hit AgentTimeoutError on step 18 without ever echoing a final move. The verifier’s first gate is file existence (test.sh:11-14).
failure_modes.evidence_quote: When you have determined the final answer, write it to `/app/answer.txt`.

counterfactual.X: Run `echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt` once the winning rook move is identified (Stockfish shallow PV d8d5 matches expected Rd5).
counterfactual.Y: Continued Stockfish re-analysis and timed out without creating /app/answer.txt.

issues: Board image uses non-standard edge labeling (h–a along the bottom, ranks 1–8 down the side) that is easy to mis-transcribe into canonical FEN.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier is a normalized string equality check against a hidden expected_answer.txt; no shortcut without the correct move.

outcome.headline: The agent timed out after building a misread FEN that Stockfish scores as roughly equal, never writing the required winning move to /app/answer.txt.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh lines 11-14 require /app/answer.txt; lines 24-34 normalize and exact-match agent answer against /tests/expected_answer.txt (expected "Rd5").
outcome.what_agent_produced: No /app/answer.txt; repeated Stockfish runs on FEN 3r2k1/pp3pp1/4b2p/7Q/3n4/PqBBR2P/5PP1/7K b - - 0 1 with deep bestmove d4f5 and ~+0.07–0.28 cp, not Rd5.

failure_modes.name: Misread board into FEN
failure_modes.description: The agent decoded the diagram with h1 as top-left and built FEN 3r2k1/pp3pp1/4b2p/7Q/3n4/PqBBR2P/5PP1/7K b - - 0 1. Stockfish on that position never shows a forced win (depth 35 bestmove d4f5, score cp 8), so the agent never converged on the required Rd5.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Top-left is h1. Bottom-right is a8.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergence before submit
failure_modes.description: After Stockfish reported a drawn evaluation (+0.08) contradicting the win requirement, the agent kept re-running engines, rotated FENs, and Lichess queries instead of writing any move to /app/answer.txt until the 600s harness timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Stockfish's evaluation of the position as drawn (+0.08).

counterfactual.X: Write the chosen algebraic move to /app/answer.txt (e.g., echo -n "Rd5" > /app/answer.txt) once identified.
counterfactual.Y: Continued Stockfish re-analysis and piece re-verification until AgentTimeoutError with no answer file written.

aggregated_from.note: Failure modes: union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Outcome/verdicts: majority vote (step_where_lost = median). Individual passes are kept under `passes`. Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
